divergence: Every mirror shows next Tuesday, and fashion has never recovered
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: Venerable Order of the Veiled Gaze Meets Its Reflective End
deck: Ceremony to Obscure an Obsolete Legacy of Unfashionable Divination
article:
In a most solemn gathering, the Venerable Order of the Veiled Gaze hath shuffled off this mortal coil, ushered into the aether with all due feudal rites by the drifting noble Aiouea, addressing an audience of precisely 437 unblinking eyes. Her eulogy rang against the bone-white spires as if they mourned their own lost chore: policing mirror veils across Kreion to avert a fashionless abyss that has now arrived.

The Order's charter, reduced to dust by sheer boredom, once demanded a weekly veil census each Wednesday. Hereditary scribes read the quotas aloud with the zeal of a forgotten errand while the procession paused three times for votes on the proper angle of collective grief.

Winding through spires twisted to frame next Tuesday's storms, the march summoned omens on the wind. Gloon the Unfathomable muttered, 'The storms shall be fierce; prepare for the cost of ignoring the veils.' The crowd nodded, already forgetting the mandates that once scheduled their Tuesdays.
author: Eldric of the Unblinking Eye, Chronicler of the Stagnant Realms

weather.condition: Overcast with a chance of reflection

oped.title: The Veiled Order's Undeserved Fate: Blame the Hats, Not the Prophets
oped.author: Lord Archibald Fustian, Eternal Curator of Obsolete Omens and Proprietary Veil Patents
oped.body: The so-called demise of the Venerable Order of the Veiled Gaze is nothing short of a tragic overreaction to Tuesday's inevitable revelations. These noble seers correctly foresaw that our current chapeaus would appear ridiculous by next week, yet instead of thanking them we dismantle their legacy. The true culprits are the victims themselves, whose ill-considered headwear choices have embarrassed divination for centuries. My own modest collection of mirrored bonnets, by contrast, remains timeless. Rather than obscure the Order's wisdom, we should mandate universal veiling at once—using, naturally, the superior fabrics I alone supply. This will restore decorum, eliminate awkward future glimpses, and incidentally secure my modest financial interests against those upstart Tuesday milliners. Fashion, after all, demands sacrifice, preferably from others.

classifieds.text: Veils once used to mask next Tuesday's sleeves, now sold cheap by a seer who needs hose that won't clash with current weather.
Seeking someone to invent mirror-free divination; the old Order's Tuesday warnings proved too accurate for comfort. Pay in today's fashion plates.
All prophecies issued by the Veiled Gaze are revoked; each only predicted next Tuesday's regrettable ruffs. Refunds available in used veil cloth.
Apologies to anyone who paid to glimpse futures that turned out to be merely Tuesdays; cloaks still available, slightly singed.

panels.caption: The Grand Seer drapes the final veil: "This rite buries the Gaze before Tuesday's sleeves betray us all."
A trembling acolyte clutches his hem: "But without the Order, how do we hide next week's disastrous ruff sizes?"
The mirror cracks anyway, exposing everyone in stained tunics: "Too late. The glass already knows we peaked on Monday."

joke.setup: The Veiled Gaze brethren, sworn never to let a looking-glass betray their humility,
joke.punchline: found every mirror already hawking next Tuesday’s ludicrous sleeves and promptly burned their habits in the square.
joke.text: The Veiled Gaze brethren, sworn never to let a looking-glass betray their humility, found every mirror already hawking next Tuesday’s ludicrous sleeves and promptly burned their habits in the square.

sponsor_ads.headline: Shards of the Shattered Order
sponsor_ads.body: Polished obsidian shards that reflect only the present hour, sparing owners the Order's fate of seeing next Tuesday's sleeves.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No returns after the 13th bell; visions may induce unseemly hose.

sponsor_ads.headline: Tuesday's Lament Veils
sponsor_ads.body: Last threads from the disbanded cloisters, woven to block mirrors that mock with garments not yet cut.

sponsor_ads.headline: Fore-Mirror Scrying Service
sponsor_ads.body: Blind seers describe the reflection you dare not see and suggest attire that will not shame you come Tuesday.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Appointments strictly before the divergence resets.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Reflective Repentance
sponsor_ads.body: Former members buy unbroken mirrors that show today instead of next week; proceeds fund sightless new habits.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Enquiries answered only on days that have already passed.

stories.headline: Council of Aeioa Mandates Gaze Aversion to Preserve Voided Prophecies
stories.deck: New canon lists Tuesday reflections among the seven forbidden sights
stories.byline: Iaoeua, Keeper of the Sacred Texts
stories.article:
The Council of Aeioa now requires gasbag adherents to veil their ocular rings before every Tuesday. The rule shields the sanctity of divinations already ruined by the mirror affliction.

Temples have been rearranged to dodge stray reflections, lifting choral resonance 47 percent across the Cobalt Drift.

stories.headline: Drift Nobility Adopts Permanent Blindfold Veils as Sole Approved Garment
stories.deck: Seasonal assemblies now convene by sound alone inside rearranged spires
stories.article:
Noble houses have outlawed reflective cloth, fining offenders with forced spire rearrangement. They drift to sound-only banquets in layered veils that hide both eyes and next week's sleeves.

Quarrels have dropped 12 percent; status now rides on tone rather than fabric.

stories.headline: Mirror Guilds of Kreion Liquidate Entire Stock After Prophecy Trade Ends
stories.deck: Warehouses emptied into spire foundations under unanimous nightly vote
stories.article: Mirror Guilds are crushing 3,400 unsold surfaces into resonators for the singing spires after demand for temporal scrying collapsed. Traders now sell veil cloth priced by ocular-ring size, not shine.

real_world_echo: Iran farewell to Khamenei as grand public mourning for an obsolete authority figure whose original purpose and relevance have faded into ritual
